title: US BIS export controls on advanced computing/semiconductors to China (initial rule)
description: US Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) issued export controls restricting China's access to advanced computing chips and semiconductor manufacturing equipment. Chip-related rulings took effect 2022-10-07, with related manufacturing-assistance rulings effective 2022-10-12 and remaining rulings effective 2022-10-21.
notes: Multiple effective dates for different rule components; 2022-10-07 recorded as the initial/primary date. Verify exact provisions against the official BIS Federal Register rule before relying on this for a specific compliance decision.

title: US BIS strengthens and closes gaps in 2022 export controls
description: BIS issued interim final rules updating the October 2022 controls, adding PRC entities to the Entity List and closing gaps that had allowed certain lower-threshold chip variants designed for the China market.
notes: Congressional Research Service summary; cross-referenced against CSIS analysis of the same rule.

title: US BIS expands controls; first country-wide HBM export restriction to China
affected_vendors: NVIDIA, AMD, SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron
description: BIS expanded controls to cover additional semiconductor manufacturing equipment and chips, restricted exports to 16 additional PRC entities, and for the first time applied a country-wide restriction on exporting advanced HBM (High-Bandwidth Memory) to China.
notes: Day-level date not confirmed from a primary source in this pass; recorded as 2024-12-01 (month precision). Verify exact date against the official Federal Register rule before relying on it.

title: US Commerce Dept. loosens restrictions, permits H200/MI325X exports to China
description: The Department of Commerce published a regulation permitting the sale of previously-restricted advanced AI chips (NVIDIA H200, AMD MI325X) to China, reversing part of the prior restriction regime.
notes: Policy reversals in this area have moved quickly and repeatedly; treat this as a point-in-time record, not necessarily still current policy.

title: TSMC CoWoS advanced packaging capacity sold out through 2025 into 2026
description: TSMC CEO C.C. Wei stated during Q3 2025 earnings that CoWoS (the advanced packaging technology required to assemble modern GPU/AI accelerator dies) capacity was sold out through 2025 and into 2026. NVIDIA is reported to hold roughly 60% of available capacity.
notes: Date recorded as approximate (Q3 2025 earnings season); exact call date not independently confirmed in this pass.

title: SK Hynix reports entire 2026 HBM supply sold out
description: SK Hynix CFO Kim Jae-joon stated the company's entire 2026 HBM (High-Bandwidth Memory) production had already been sold out, underscoring HBM as the tightest single component constraint in AI accelerator supply chains.
notes: Exact statement date approximate; sourced via a secondary aggregator/newsletter rather than a direct SK Hynix earnings call transcript.
